works.name: Ho System
works.short_description: A published open methodology for human-AI collaborative software development in which the practitioner keeps full architectural authority and uses AI as an implementation partner.
substantive_description: The dominant frame for AI-assisted development assumes the model leads: the human gestures, the model generates, the human accepts or rejects. The Ho System refuses that position. It treats AI as an implementation partner inside a discipline the human owns — architectural authority held, structural decisions made consciously, every session bounded, every artifact reviewed. The working unit is a ho (歩, "step"): a bounded, roughly two-hour session that produces something real, connects to the project arc by commit hash, and closes with structured reflection; practitioners progress through Shu-Ha-Ri stages, and a tiered-understanding frame — Black Box, Functional, Deep — keeps an honest account of what is understood versus delegated. The deeper claim is about agency in the age of capable models: those models tempt the practitioner to delegate exactly the work that builds judgment, and the Ho System is the operational answer — a methodology that lets you ship without becoming someone who cannot ship alone. Eight production systems have shipped under it, including a computer-vision platform running at Harvard and a ZFS backup orchestrator with 278 tests at 99% coverage.

The Ho System is a methodology rather than a codebase: it ships as an open framework of documents, templates, and operating discipline, published under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0, with a methodology site at atmarcus.net/ho and the framework repository on GitHub; it is also taught as a navigable graph through Ho System, Actually, a live instance of the *, Actually engine at star-actually.sageframe.net/ho-actually.

state.last: **ho-12 CLOSED** (2026-07-12, the ho-status companion). All five agent tasks done: `654d9b2` (AT-01) title/description required frontmatter; `249df89` (AT-02) ho-status doctrine in kamae-project-framing §2.4 (json schema + single `state` enum + md render + root `metadata/` zone + inherited visibility); `d632483` (AT-03) skill/scaffolder/template wiring — K5 gains the roster refresh as a **fifth ho-close move**, K4 notes the companion, scaffolder seeds root `metadata/`, all installed `~/.claude` copies synced by insertion; AT-04 (local) the keisaku handoff prompt (`ho-process/agent-tasks/keisaku-metadata-handoff.md`); `be7a3c9` (AT-05) ho-system's own reference roster `metadata/ho-status.{json,md}` + title/description backfill + `metadata/` gitignore. Building the reference instance forced two corrections: **ho-06 closed retrospectively** (work shipped 2026-07-03 but the ho was never closed — Results unfilled, status draft) and **ho-07 added to the roster** (complete + documented but unphased in K4). Roster now **14/14 complete**; the fifth close-move dogfooded itself on ho-12's own close.
state.next: **ho-13** — the generator + cross-project dashboard. Opens on IDEA-013 (the input contract): the generator takes its ho census from a `ho-process/hos/*.md` glob + frontmatter (add a **`phase:` field** so grouping is self-described), using K4 only for phase order + release tags — never parsing K4 prose. Doc-less-but-complete hos (ho-08) need a reconciliation path. Then ho-14 = fleet rollout (Sonnet titles, Haiku generation).
